items.title: tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
items.evidence: tsc exited clean during execution verification

items.title: check:conventions --strict has no new violations
items.evidence: CLEAR structural violations — backend (size + console + db): 0

items.title: Dev real send delivers via Mailgun with provider_message_id
items.precondition: Deployed to dev with MAILGUN_API_KEY + MAILGUN_DOMAIN + EMAIL_DEFAULT_TRANSPORT=mailgun set in dev.env; DNS-verified sending domain
steps: 1. Trigger an email send on dev (e.g. support reply, contact form, or admin notification)
2. Check email_logs table for the new row
3. Verify provider_message_id is a normalized Mailgun id (no angle brackets)
items.expected: email_logs row has status=sent, provider_message_id like 'xxxx@email.getalloro.com', email arrives in inbox
items.notes: Requires Mailgun DNS-verified domain and dev deploy
items.waiver: Owner-waived: will verify on dev after this push deploys. Env vars already staged on both servers.

items.title: Fallback: EMAIL_DEFAULT_TRANSPORT=n8n sends via n8n unchanged
items.precondition: Set EMAIL_DEFAULT_TRANSPORT=n8n in env, ALLORO_EMAIL_SERVICE_WEBHOOK configured
steps: 3. Verify the email routes through n8n webhook
items.expected: Email sent via n8n path, no Mailgun calls
items.notes: Can be verified by checking email.log for 'Sending email via n8n'
items.waiver: Owner-waived: fallback path preserved in code and tested via unit tests (resolveTransport); live verification deferred to post-deploy.

items.title: No new migration; email_logs schema unchanged
steps: 1. Check git diff for any new files in database/migrations/
2. Verify no schema changes to email_logs
items.expected: Zero new migration files; email_logs table untouched
items.evidence: No files created in database/migrations/; EmailLogModel unchanged

items.title: (Rev 2) Admin sends test email from modal, sees log row
items.precondition: Deployed to dev with Mailgun configured; logged in as super-admin
steps: 2. Click 'Send Test' button in the header
3. Type a recipient email address in the modal
5. Confirm success state shows transport name
6. Close modal and verify a new row appears in the logs table with category=system
items.expected: Modal shows success with transport=mailgun, logs table has a new 'system' row with the test email subject, email arrives at recipient
items.notes: Requires dev deploy with Mailgun configured
items.waiver: Owner-waived: test-send modal built and compiles; live UI verification deferred to post-deploy.
